location.office_time_required: up to one day per week from home (predominantly in-office)

secondary_archetype: Program Manager (investment product management / asset management product)

level_signals: 5+ years of total relevant work experience required
May provide guidance to more junior associates
Serves as backup to senior product managers
Comp range $96,500–$205,000 depending on location, consistent with mid-senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or the equivalent combination of education and relevant experience AND
5+ years of total relevant work experience

Conducts product development and enhancement activities relating to the US equity suite of products. Establishes relationships with operations, technology, risk, compliance, legal, etc., to ensure requirements are met.
Manages multiple projects, monitoring interdependencies and synergies where required, and enabling effective communication of critical milestones occurring.
Finds and transforms data into insightful analysis and then clearly and succinctly communicate those conclusions.
Demonstrates knowledge of assigned products, the marketplace, client needs, and the operational/regulatory environment.
Leverage AI tools to enhance workflow efficiency and data analysis within daily responsibilities.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Knowledge of T. Rowe Price equity investment strategies, markets, and competitive offerings
Strong communication skills (both verbal and written), demonstrating the ability to influence people at various levels of the organization
Strong analytical skills with ability to gather meaningful data and synthesize it to represent a story
Proficiency in Microsoft Suite, including Excel, CoPilot, and PowerPoint, with ability to create/edit presentations and experience leveraging functions and data analysis tools
Experience in third party investment data providers a plus (e.g. Morningstar Direct, eVestment)
Professional curiosity, initiative, and creativity to identify and pursue new opportunities and problem resolutions
Enthusiastic and optimistic with a drive to succeed and willingness to take managed risks
Ability to manage time and shifting priorities effectively while in a challenging operating environment

explicitly_excluded: FINRA licenses are not required and will not be supported for this role.

scope.surface: US equity suite of products within the Global Product organization
scope.business_model: B2B and B2C asset management (institutional, advisor, retail, retirement plan sponsor clients)
primary_metrics_inferred: Product health metrics across equity strategies/products/channels/vehicles
Key metrics monitoring across equity suite
core_responsibilities: Lead product management and development projects relating to the TRP equity suite of products
Coordinate Equity Product Team activities and meetings
Reporting and communication around key metrics
Represent the team on various cross functional initiatives
Support product changes, enhancements, launches, firm initiatives, and potential new business requests
Contribute to development of strategic plans and review of product health
Support reporting and key metrics monitoring across the equity suite of products
Leverage AI tools to enhance workflow efficiency and data analysis

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated across multiple geographies ($96,500–$205,000 base)
Generous benefits package described (retirement plan, health, PTO, family care)
Clear team and product surface described (equity suite within Global Product org)
85+ year established firm with stable institutional backing
Role offers exposure to cross-functional initiatives and senior product manager backup responsibilities
red_flags: Hybrid policy is predominantly in-office — 'up to one day per week from home' means ~4 days in office; heavily restricts remote candidates
Most qualifications (communication, analytical skills, curiosity) are listed as 'Preferred' rather than required, making the actual bar ambiguous
FINRA licenses explicitly not supported, which may limit career mobility within financial services

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + comp range + people-guidance language)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as asset manager serving institutions, advisors, retail, retirement plan sponsors)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly named in JD)
location.cities_listed (only Baltimore, MD listed in URL; JD body references multiple comp geographies including MD, CO, WA, DC, NY, CA but does not confirm all as work locations)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from mention of 'remote workers' in base salary ranges section)
ambiguities: Work location is listed as Baltimore, MD in the URL, but the compensation section mentions Maryland, Colorado, Washington, D.C., New York, California, and 'remote workers' — it is unclear whether all these are eligible work locations or purely pay-band references. Treated as Baltimore-primary with remote-eligible flag set true given explicit 'remote workers' comp tier.

Most skills labeled 'Preferred' in the JD could reasonably be considered de facto requirements for success in this role (e.g. strong communication, analytical skills, Microsoft Suite proficiency). Classified as nice_to_haves per JD language, but ambiguity noted.
Hybrid policy states 'up to one day per week from home' — this is interpreted as predominantly onsite (~4 days/week in office), not a standard hybrid split.
